name: AI Auto-Tagging
author: RFLundgren
description: Auto-classifies tracks (genre, mood, language, etc.) using an AI provider so the library becomes filterable by AI-suggested tags

scheduler.reason: Periodically scan the library for tracks to classify

subsonicapi.reason: Read track metadata (search3) and read/write user tags (getUserTags/setUserTag)

users.reason: Required alongside subsonicapi - identifies which user's library view and tags the plugin acts as

kvstore.reason: Track a high-water mark so scheduled runs only scan tracks not yet covered

taskqueue.reason: Batch-process track classification in the background, with retries and a concurrency cap

http.reason: Call the configured AI provider's classification API

provider.title: AI Provider
provider.description: Which AI provider to call for classification

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.description: API key for the selected provider

model.title: Model
model.description: Model name for the selected provider (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5, gpt-5-mini, gemini-flash)

tagCategories.title: Tag Categories
tagCategories.description: Which categories to suggest tags for

genreVocabulary.title: Genre Vocabulary
genreVocabulary.description: Comma-separated list of genre words the AI is allowed to use. Pre-filled with the built-in defaults - add or remove entries as you like. Keeping this to a fixed, curated list (rather than open-ended AI word choice) avoids near-duplicate tags like 'hip hop' vs 'hiphop' vs 'rap' across separate classification runs.
genreVocabulary.default: rock, pop, electronic, hip hop, jazz, classical, metal, folk, country, r&b, soul, blues, reggae, punk, indie, ambient, new age, world, funk, disco, house, techno, alternative, soundtrack, experimental

moodVocabulary.title: Mood Vocabulary
moodVocabulary.description: Comma-separated list of mood words the AI is allowed to use. Pre-filled with the built-in defaults - add or remove entries as you like. Keeping this to a fixed, curated list (rather than open-ended AI word choice) avoids near-duplicate tags like 'chill' vs 'relaxed' vs 'mellow' across separate classification runs.
moodVocabulary.default: happy, chill, energetic, melancholy, party, aggressive, romantic, dreamy, dark, uplifting, nostalgic, peaceful

libraryUser.title: Library User
libraryUser.description: Username whose library view is used to read tracks and read/write tags

cron.title: Scan Schedule
cron.description: Cron expression for how often to scan the library (default: daily at 03:00)

maxTracksPerRun.title: Max Tracks Per Run
maxTracksPerRun.description: Cost/time control ceiling on how many tracks are scanned per scheduled run
